steps.title: Identify the stated goal
steps.content: The student’s goal is to emphasize the location of Hornsdale Wind Farm. The relevant note is: "Hornsdale Wind Farm is located in Hornsdale, Australia." The correct choice must put location at the forefront — not capacity, definitions, or general wind-turbine facts.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Hornsdale Wind Farm has a nameplate capacity of 316 megawatts.': Emphasizes the farm’s electrical output, not its location. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Nameplate capacity is another name for a wind farm’s maximum electrical output.': Defines a technical term; says nothing about where Hornsdale Wind Farm is. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Hornsdale, Australia, is home to Hornsdale Wind Farm.': Places the location — Hornsdale, Australia — in the grammatical subject position, making it the focal point of the sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Turbines can be used to generate electricity from a renewable energy source: the wind.': Explains how wind turbines work in general; does not mention where Hornsdale Wind Farm is located. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
